finding: A matrix label or workflow status is insufficient by itself. Acceptance must bind Linux and macOS observations to one exact commit, require the complete 12-cycle contract on each platform, require zero residual coordination artifacts, and retain digests for runner identity and raw logs.
design_implication: Queue state may advance from implemented_runtime_pending only after the classifier accepts a complete two-platform packet. Missing, duplicate, cross-commit, failed, or dirty observations remain unresolved or falsifying evidence.
adversarial_cases: duplicate Linux evidence presented as a two-platform matrix
verification: Committed deterministic test contract; runtime tests were not executed in this connector-only run.
falsification_route: Reject if either platform is absent, duplicated, points to another commit, has a nonzero exit code, completes other than 12 cycles, records other than 12 terminal outcomes, leaves a residual coordination artifact, or lacks artifact names and SHA-256 digests.
epistemic_limit: Acceptance proves only the bounded contract on retained runner observations for the exact commit.
